steps.title: Find the width of the rectangular base
steps.content:
The base area is the product of length and width. Given base area = $16t$ cm² and length = $\frac{8}{3}$ cm:

$$16t = \frac{8}{3} \cdot w$$

Multiply both sides by $\frac{3}{8}$ to isolate $w$:

$$w = 16t \cdot \frac{3}{8} = \frac{48t}{8} = 6t \text{ cm}$$

steps.title: Calculate surface area from all three face types
steps.content:
A rectangular prism has three pairs of rectangular faces. The surface area is:

$$SA = 2(lw) + 2(lh) + 2(wh)$$

With $l = \frac{8}{3}$ cm, $w = 6t$ cm, and $h = 9$ cm:

  • Two bases: $2 \times 16t = 32t$ cm²
  • Two length-height faces: $2 \times \frac{8}{3} \times 9 = 2 \times 24 = 48$ cm²
  • Two width-height faces: $2 \times 6t \times 9 = 108t$ cm²

$$SA = 32t + 48 + 108t = 140t + 48 \text{ cm}^2$$

That matches choice B — B is correct.
